<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Subscription Plan</th><th>Conversion Rate Sheet</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Automation Starter<br>Automation Advanced<br>Automation for vCore/Core<br>MuleSoft Integration Starter<br>MuleSoft Integration Advanced<br>MuleSoft Integration Unlimited<br>MuleSoft for Flow Integration - Enterprise Edition<br>MuleSoft for Flow Integration - Performance Edition<br>MuleSoft for Flow Integration - Unlimited Edition</p></td><td><p><a href="mulesoft-automation-credits-3-0">MuleSoft Automation Credits 3.0</a></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Automation Starter<br>Automation Advanced<br>Automation for vCore/Core<br>Additional Automation Credits</p></td><td><p><a href="mulesoft-automation-credits-2-0">MuleSoft Automation Credits 2.0</a></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>MuleSoft - Automation<br>MuleSoft - Automation - (Gold, Platinum, Titanium Edition)<br>MuleSoft - Automation for Salesforce<br>MuleSoft - Automation for Salesforce - (Gold, Platinum, Titanium Edition)<br>MuleSoft - Automation - Additional Credits (100)<br>MuleSoft - Automation - Additional Credits (100) - (Gold, Platinum, Titanium)</p></td><td><p><a href="mulesoft-automation-credits-1-0">MuleSoft Automation Credits 1.0</a></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## MuleSoft Automation Credits

A quantity of Automation Credits is included with certain MuleSoft Services, with the specific
entitlement indicated on the Usage Details table on the Order Form for such Services. Automation
Credits are consumed as you create and execute automations based on the rates in the following
table. While MuleSoft Automation Credits 1.0 uses a conversion-based model, MuleSoft Automation
Credits 2.0 and 3.0 use multiplier rates. Credit usage rates differ by subscription plan. Refer to
the column that corresponds to your credit system.

> [!IMPORTANT] As of February 18, 2026, Flow Orchestration is included for customers in Enterprise
> and higher editions. For customers on other editions, the
> [General Flow Limits](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=platform.flow_considerations_limit.htm&type=5)
> apply.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Usage Type</th><th>MuleSoft Automation Credits 3.0</th><th>MuleSoft Automation Credits 2.0</th><th>MuleSoft Automation Credits 1.0</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Visibility and Reporting</strong></p></td><td><p><strong>Digital Wallet:</strong> Automation Credits 3.0 provides an instant visibility of credit consumption.</p></td><td><p><strong>Anypoint Reports (Monthly):</strong> Usage aggregated and reported via Sequoia on a monthly cycle.</p></td><td><p><strong>Anypoint Reports (Monthly):</strong> Legacy reporting via Sequoia based on monthly usage aggregates.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Flow Orchestration Runs</strong></p></td><td><p><strong>As of February 18, 2026, for Enterprise and Higher Editions</strong>: Included<br>For customers on other editions, the <a href="https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=platform.flow_considerations_limit.htm&amp;type=5" target="_blank" rel="noopener">General Flow Limits</a> apply.</p></td><td><p>100 automation credits per run.<br><strong>As of February 18, 2026, for Enterprise and Higher Editions</strong>: Included<br>For customers on other editions, the <a href="https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=platform.flow_considerations_limit.htm&amp;type=5" target="_blank" rel="noopener">General Flow Limits</a> apply.</p></td><td><p>Not Available</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>IDP Document Pages</strong></p></td><td><p>30 credits per page.<br>Usage calculated using a multiplier of each page. A page is defined as a single physical or digital page within a processed document. This enables you to track real-time visibility into document volumes processed via the Salesforce-integrated dashboard.</p></td><td><p>30 credits per page.<br>Usage calculated using a multiplier of each processed page. This usage includes AI-driven classification and data extraction.</p></td><td><p>5 pages per credit.<br>Usage calculated based on allowance in which 1 credit entitles you to 5 pages.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Integration Tasks</strong></p></td><td><p>2 credits per task.<br>Usage calculated based on the execution of discrete tasks. This enables an immediate monitoring of the task consumption.</p></td><td><p>2 credits per successful task.<br>Usage calculated based on the execution of discrete tasks within a MuleSoft integration flow.</p></td><td><p>Not Available</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>MuleSoft for Flow: IDP</strong></p></td><td><p>30 credits per page.<br>This usage type tracks IDP pages processed specifically when triggered from within a Salesforce Flow via the native MuleSoft integration.</p></td><td><p>Not Available</p></td><td><p>Not Available</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>RPA Bot Minutes</strong></p></td><td><p>Not Available</p></td><td><p>80 credits per RPA Bot minute.<br>Usage calculated by multiplying the raw execution minutes by a constant multiplier.</p></td><td><p>2 RPA Bot minutes per credit.<br>Usage calculated by summing the total execution time (in seconds) of all RPA processes over the subscription term. This total is converted to minutes and then to credits. MuleSoft adds all the RPA execution times in seconds for the period and converts the total into minutes with two decimal precision.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>RPA API Calls</strong></p></td><td><p>Not Available</p></td><td><p>2 credits per successful call to the RPA Manager or RPA Bot API.<br>Usage calculated per individual request.</p></td><td><p>100 Bot API calls per credit.<br>Usage calculated by summing the total calls made to the RPA Manager or RPA Bot API to trigger processes or retrieve status.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Composer Tasks</strong></p></td><td><p>Not Available</p></td><td><p>2 credits per task.<br>Usage calculated using a multiplier in which each successful connector action consumes 2 credits.</p></td><td><p>50 tasks per credit.<br>Usage calculated based on allowance in which 1 credit entitles you to 50 tasks. A task is any successful action performed by a connector.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

> [!NOTE] Quoting rules generally prevent you from holding both MuleSoft Automation credits 2.0 and
> 3.0 simultaneously. However, if you have both MuleSoft Automation credits 2.0 and 3.0, the system
> sends all usage data to the Digital Wallet to provide a single, centralized view of consumption.

## Viewing Usage and Billing

The method for viewing usage and handling billing varies based on the type of Automation purchase.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Usage Type</th><th>Usage Visibility</th><th>Billing (monthly statements and overage)</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>MuleSoft Automation Credits 3.0</p></td><td><p>Digital Wallet (and optionally Anypoint Usage Reports if you have an Anypoint Platform org)</p></td><td><p>Digital Wallet</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>MuleSoft Automation Credits 2.0</p></td><td><p>Anypoint Usage Reports</p></td><td><p>Sequoia</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>MuleSoft Automation Credits 1.0</p></td><td><p>Anypoint Usage Reports</p></td><td><p>Sequoia</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Monthly Account Summary Emails

Salesforce sends usage-based billing reports to every MuleSoft Composer, Intelligent Document
Processing (IDP), and Robotic Process Automation (RPA) customer on the 10th day of each month. By
default, Salesforce sends these emails to the accountâs billing contact.

For more information about the reports, the list of recipients, and other details, see the
[Monthly Account Summary Email FAQ](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=000390885&type=1).

## See Also

- [MuleSoft Automation Credits 3.0](mulesoft-automation-credits-3-0)
- [MuleSoft Automation Credits 2.0](mulesoft-automation-credits-2-0)
- [MuleSoft Automation Credits 1.0](mulesoft-automation-credits-1-0)
- [Digital WalletâEnabled Consumption-Based Products](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=xcloud.wallet_enabled_products_rate_cards.htm&type=5)
  (Salesforce Help)
---

# Browser Support for Anypoint Platform

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Use Anypoint Platform with the latest version of any supported browser:

- Microsoft Edge with Chromium
- Microsoft Edge without Chromium (supported through December 31, 2020)
- Google Chrome
- Mozilla Firefox
- Safari

Browser support for Anypoint Platform follows the Salesforce Lightning Experience policy.

## Exceptions to Browser Support Policy

Partner Manager 2.0 modifies the browser support policy:

- [Partner Manager 2.0](../release-notes/partner-manager/anypoint-partner-manager-2.0-release-notes#browser-compatibility)

## Unsupported Browsers

Internet Explorer versions 11 and earlier are not supported. If you use the browser with Anypoint
Platform, you might experience issues.

## See Also

[Supported Browsers for Lightning Experience](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?language=en_US&id=getstart_browsers_sfx.htm&type=5)
---

# Contribute to MuleSoft Documentation

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Many of our documentation repositories are open and we welcome your contributions!

To contribute to the English version of this documentation:

1.  If this is your first time contributing to MuleSoft documentation, sign the
    [Salesforce.com Contributor License Agreement (CLA)](https://cla.salesforce.com/sign-cla). You
    need to sign the CLA only once per GitHub account.
2.  Navigate to the page that you want to modify and click **View in Github**.
3.  Make your edits.
4.  Submit a pull request to the GitHub repository.

The Documentation team then either accepts your pull request or contacts you for clarification.

If your pull request results in an error, verify that you signed the CLA in Step 1.
---

# Upgrading Java for Custom Connectors (Customers)

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Standard support for Java 8 and 11 ends in August 2026 for 4.6 LTS. Plan your upgrade path for apps
that are running on Java 8 or 11 accordingly.

> [!NOTE] This guide applies to MuleSoft customers only. If you are a MuleSoft partner, see
> [Upgrading Java for Custom Connectors (Partners)](partner-connector-upgrade).

Upgrade, test, and release your custom connectors for Java 17 to ensure compatibility within the
MuleSoft ecosystem. Ensuring this compatibility is crucial because running an incompatible connector
can lead to runtime failures, decreased performance, and security vulnerabilities that could impact
your appâs stability.

## Before You Begin

Before upgrading and testing your custom connector for Java 17, you must be familiar with the
following considerations:

- Target compilation level

  In Mule 4.9.0 and later, you can compile any Mule project for Java 17 or lower. Additional
  dependencies must be compiled for Java 17 or lower.

  If you compile for Java 17, upgrade the `mule-modules-parent` to version 1.9.0 or later. The
  connectorâs minimum Mule version will also be 4.9.0, ensuring compatibility only with Mule 4.9.0
  or later. Verify that `minMuleVersion` in `META-INF/mule-artifact/mule-artifact.json` is 4.9.0 or
  later. See
  [Specify Java Compatibility](../mule-sdk/latest/java-version-support#specify-java-compatibility).

  If the `mule-modules-parent` version is earlier than 1.9.0, the bytecode level for the connector
  and its additional dependencies must be for Java 8. You canât use language features from Java 11
  or Java 17.

- Unsupported directives

  Donât set `--add-opens`, `--add-exports`, or `--add-modules` directives. These directives are not
  allowed in CloudHub, Runtime Fabric, or on-premises. These directives also require managing them
  for each connector across all environments, and introduce a backward compatibility burden,
  complicating future Java upgrades and potentially enabling security vulnerabilities. If these
  directives are used, they are not covered under Support.

- Minimum Mule version

  If your custom connector is running on Mule 4.5.x or earlier, you canât run Java 17 tests that
  extend the `MuleArtifactFunctionalTestCase`, but you can still run Java 8 tests.

  To decouple the minimum Mule version when running tests, add the `-Dmunit.jvm` parameter to use
  Java 17 as described in
  [Test Your Custom Connector with MUnit](#test-your-custom-connector-with-munit).

## Upgrade Your Custom Mule SDK Connectors

Upgrade your custom Mule SDK (Java SDK and XML SDK) connectors to Java 17.

### Upgrade Third-Party Libraries

Your custom connector likely relies on third-party libraries, so itâs important to ensure they are
compatible with Java 17 as well. Even if your custom connector passes tests, it is still important
to deliver secure and reliable software. If a critical bug or vulnerability is found, you must
upgrade to a fixed version. Using up-to-date libraries that explicitly support Java 17 makes this
process easier and more reliable, as the vendor has already tested them on Java 17.

Upgrade the third-party libraries of your custom connector to be compatible with Java 17:

- Java EE libraries

  Mule 4.6.x exposes Java EE libraries differently than earlier Mule versions. You must add the BOM
  dependency to your custom connector and, if applicable, exclude the provided conflicting library.
  You can also add the libraries in the BOM dependency separately.

  ```java
  <properties>
   <muleJavaEeBomVersion>4.6.0</muleJavaEeBomVersion>
  </properties>
  <dependencyManagement>
   <dependencies>
     <dependency>
       <groupId>org.mule</groupId>
       <artifactId>mule-javaee-runtime-bom</artifactId>
       <version>${muleJavaEeBomVersion}</version>
       <type>pom</type>
       <scope>import</scope>
     </dependency>
   </dependencies>
  </dependencyManagement>
  ```

  For more information, see [Java EE Libraries](../mule-sdk/latest/java-ee-libraries).

- External libraries

  Upgrade external libraries, which are third-party libraries that are not bundled with your custom
  connector. Some examples include a JDBC driver, JMS broker client, or Groovy runtime. The
  following example shows a JDBC driver external library configuration:

  ```java
  @ExternalLib(name = "MySQL JDBC Driver",
  description = "A JDBC driver that supports connection to the MySQL Database",
  nameRegexpMatcher = "(.*)\\.jar",
  requiredClassName = "com.mysql.jdbc.Driver",
  coordinates = "mysql:mysql-connector-java:5.1.44")
  public class MySqlConnectionProvider implements ConnectionProvider<Connection> {
    //
  }
  ```

  You must update your external libraries to a version that is compatible with Java 17.

### Review Your Code

After you compile your custom connector, ensure you can run your custom connector with Java 17.
Reviewing your code is important because some restrictions are flagged as warnings in Java 11 but
are flagged as failures and canât be disabled in Java 17. For example, reflection is more restricted
in Java 17, so you must review your code to ensure there are no illegal actions.

To help you review your code, learn more about the different changes in Java 17:

- JDK Migration Guide

  See the
  [Oracle JDK Migration Guide](https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/migrate/getting-started.html#GUID-C25E2B1D-6C24-4403-8540-CFEA875B994A)
  for a full list of the changes made from Java 8 to Java 17. Use the guide to assess the impact to
  your code.

- Mule runtime engine

  See [Mule runtime](java-support#mule-runtime) to learn more about the Java 17 changes made in Mule
  runtime engine.
  - Reflection on Java classes

    To ensure compatibility with Java 17, review and update any code that uses reflection to access
    Java classes or set parameter values. Java 17 imposes stricter access controls, so avoid
    introspecting JDK internals. Have a robust test suite that runs with Java 17. Pay special
    attention to the `setAccessible(true)` method because these access changes are not allowed in
    Java 17.

  - Serialization

    Update your serialization code to accommodate the restrictions of Java 17. If your code uses
    libraries like Gson for serializing classes, switch to using Data Transfer Objects (DTOs) where
    reflective access to JDK classes is involved. Ensure that serialization libraries are updated
    and adjust your code to minimize reliance on reflection. For more information, refer to
    [Configure Custom Serializers](../mule-runtime/latest/configure-custom-serializers).

  - Library upgrades

    Upgrade your libraries to versions compatible with Java 17. This includes updating ByteBuddy to
    version 1.14.0, Jacoco to 0.8.10, and SLF4J to version 2.x. Replace CGLib with ByteBuddy and
    check for updates to other libraries like Groovy and JRuby. Verify that all dependencies are
    compatible with Java 17 to avoid runtime issues.

  - Changes with running the test suite

    Adapt your testing approach for Java 17. If you use Mockito, be aware that Mockito canât mock
    JVM classes. Consider custom implementations for mocking and switch from PowerMock to newer
    Mockito versions. Update your tests to handle the changes in Java 17 and avoid deprecated
    methods.

  - Java Platform Module System (JPMS)

    JPMS was introduced in Java 9. This feature introduces stricter encapsulation, impacting how
    Mule modules interact with JDK classes. As Mule upgrades to Java 17 and JPMS, these changes are
    necessary:
    - Refactor Split Packages: Resolve issues with internal and API package splits by reorganizing
      and refactoring packages to conform with JPMS modularization standards.
    - Address `ResourceBundle` Loading: Implement solutions for issues related to resource bundle
      loading, such as using `ResourceBundleProvider` or alternative methods.
    - Analyze the third-party libraries' changelogs to understand Java 17 compatibility and changes
      related to JPMS.

### Add Missing Code

Because reflection is more restricted in Java 17, API objects now require setters. Previously with
Java 8, it was enough for API objects to only have default constructors and getters for all
DataWeave accessible properties. Now, API objects must also have setters so DataWeave can write
those properties without using reflection.

Constructors and setters are required if your class is instantiated by DataWeave, and getters are
required if your class is read. If your class is returned and not instantiated, only getters are
required. However, using both getters and setters simplifies the validation and certification
process.

## Upgrade Your Custom REST Connect Connectors

Upgrade your custom REST Connect connectors to Java 17.

REST Connect now supports Java 17. REST Connect connectors are generated from an API specification
using REST Connect. To make the connector Java 17-compatible, republish the API specification to
Exchange. See [REST Connect Connector Generator](../exchange/to-deploy-using-rest-connect).

> [!IMPORTANT] REST Connect now adds support for TLS. To save time, enable TLS at the same time you
> update your generated connector for Java 17 so that you need to generate the connector and test
> your apps only once.

## Upgrade Your Custom Configuration Properties Providers

To upgrade your custom configuration properties provider to be compatible with Java 17, switch to
use [Java SDK](../mule-sdk/latest/getting-started) and use the `@JavaVersionSupport` annotation as
explained in [Release Your Custom Connector](#release-your-custom-connector). You must also perform
all the steps described in
[Upgrading Java for Custom Connectors (Customers)](customer-connector-upgrade), just like with any
custom connector. The example custom configuration properties provider mentioned in
[Example: Mule SDK Module](../mule-runtime/latest/custom-configuration-properties-provider#example-mule-sdk-module)
is updated to support Java 17. Refer to that example to update your custom configuration properties
provider. For more details about what the changed code looks like (including migrating tests to
MUnit), refer to this
[changeset](https://github.com/mulesoft/mule-custom-properties-providers-module-example/commit/410ee26d8b3c44adacb077e099f32886f99eb6be).

Alternatively, if switching to using [Java SDK](../mule-sdk/latest/getting-started) involves too
many changes for you, add a declaration using `ExtensionDeclarer` inside `ExtensionLoadingDelegate`
in your custom configuration properties provider. For more details about what the changed code looks
like, refer to this
[changeset](https://github.com/mulesoft/mule-custom-properties-providers-module-example/commit/19d9d45cd52b0695f7b9c2b9019bae88f45fb228).

## Test Your Custom Connector with MUnit

Run your MUnit tests to test compatibility of your connector against Java 17. Ensure your local JDK
version is 17.

1.  Open the pom.xml file of your Mule app.
2.  Replace the `mule-maven-plugin` version with the `${mule.maven.plugin.version}` parameter.
3.  If you havenât already, add the `munit-maven-plugin`. Replace the version with the
    `${munit.version}` parameter and replace `runtimeVersion` with the `${app.runtime}` parameter.

    ```xml
    	<plugin>
    		<groupId>com.mulesoft.munit.tools</groupId>
    		<artifactId>munit-maven-plugin</artifactId>
    		<version>${munit.version}</version>
    		<executions>
    			<execution>
    				<id>test</id>
    				<phase>test</phase>
    				<goals>
    					<goal>test</goal>
    					<goal>coverage-report</goal>
    				</goals>
    			</execution>
    		</executions>
    		<configuration>
    			<coverage>
    				<runCoverage>true</runCoverage>
    				<formats>
    					<format>html</format>
    				</formats>
    			</coverage>
    			<runtimeVersion>${app.runtime}</runtimeVersion>
    			<dynamicPorts>
    				<dynamicPort>http.port</dynamicPort>
    			</dynamicPorts>
    		</configuration>
    	</plugin>
    ```

    > [!NOTE] MUnit 3.1 is compatible only with Mule runtime 4.3.0 and later. If your connector is
    > compatible with Mule runtime 4.2.0 and earlier, you must create a legacy profile that
    > overrides the MUnit version.

4.  If you have MUnit dependencies, such as `munit-runner` and `munit-tools`, replace the version
    for each dependency with the `${munit-version}` parameter.
5.  Replace the version for each connector dependency with the Java 17 compatible version of the
    connector.
6.  Open a terminal window in the root of your Mule project and run the following command. This
    command displays the earliest versions of Mule runtime, MUnit, and the Mule Maven plugin that
    are compatible with Java 17.

    ```bash
    mvn -f pom.xml -s ~/.m2/settings.xml -Dapp.runtime=4.6.0 -Dmunit.version=3.1.0 -Dmule.maven.plugin.version=4.1.0 -fae test
    ```

You can now see if your connector is compatible with Java 17. For more information about running
MUnit tests, refer to [MUnit](../munit/latest/).

The Mule runtime version you use determines the version of the mule-modules-parent. For example, if
you use Mule runtime 4.6.0, you must use mule-modules-parent 1.6.0. Minor versions maintain a
correspondence, such as Mule runtime 4.1.0 with mule-modules parent 1.1.0, Mule runtime 4.2.0 with
mule-modules-parent 1.2.0, and so forth.

Java 17 is supported with Mule runtime 4.6.0 and later. However, a connector can be compatible with
both Mule 4.3.0 and Java 17 simultaneously. If your connector must be compatible with Mule 4.3.0,
its mule-modules-parent version cannot exceed 1.3.0. You donât necessarily need to use
mule-modules-parent 1.6.0 for your connector to be compatible with Java 17. Using
mule-modules-parent 1.6.0 is specifically required to leverage other features from the Mule runtime
4.6.0 in the connector.

## Release Your Custom Connector

After you update your code and your tests are green, you are ready to release a new Java
17-compatible version of your custom connector.

1.  To communicate Java 17 compatibility, generate metadata for Java compatibility of your custom
    connector by adding or upgrading the custom connector `mule-sdk-api` dependency to the latest
    version:

    ```xml
    <dependency>
       <groupId>org.mule.sdk</groupId>
       <artifactId>mule-sdk-api</artifactId>
       <version>0.10.1</version>
    </dependency>
    ```

2.  For Java SDK, add the `@JavaVersionSupport` annotation in the same class as the `@Extension`
    annotation and include the `JAVA_17` value, for example:

    > [!NOTE] You donât need to add any annotations for XML SDK because XML SDK modules are Java 17
    > compatible and inherit the property automatically.

    ```java
    @Extension(name = "Database")
    @Operations(...)
    @JavaVersionSupport({JAVA_8, JAVA_11, JAVA_17})
    public class DatabaseConnector {
    ..
    }
    ```

In Mule 4.5.0 and later, custom connectors that donât specify the `@JavaVersionSupport` annotation
are assumed to be compatible with Java 8 and Java 11.

You can mark your custom connector as compatible with Java 17 only; however, you must ensure that no
adoption or backward compatibility issues exist.

When you deploy a Mule app, Mule verifies that all modules in the Mule app are compatible with the
Java version. If Mule finds an incompatibility, Mule throws an error and the application does not
deploy.

> [!NOTE] If you receive an error message specific to an XML SDK based connector, such as
> `Extension 'module-error-handler-plugin' does not support Java 17. Supported versions are: [1.8, 11]`,
> this means that your Mule app still contains some connectors that are not compatible with Java 17.
> To resolve this error, upgrade all connectors in your Mule app to be compatible with Java 17.

If your code is compatible with Java 17 but you donât declare Java 17 compatibility, you can still
get a successful test run.

To run a quick check on your custom connector or if all dependencies are not ready, pass the
following argument to skip hard checks on the Java support declaration:

```bash
-M-Dmule.jvm.version.extension.enforcement=LOOSE
```

For more information, see [Java Version Support](../mule-sdk/latest/java-version-support).

## See Also

- [Java Support](java-support)
---

# Troubleshoot the AI Assistant

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

If you experience issues using the Ask MuleSoft AI assistant, use this guide to identify and resolve
common problems.

## AI Assistant Doesnât Respond

If the AI assistant doesnât respond to your messages:

Check your connection:

1.  Verify that you have an active internet connection.
2.  Refresh the browser page.
3.  Try sending a simple message like "Hello" to test if the assistant responds.

Check session status:

1.  Verify that youâre still signed in to the enhanced experience.
2.  If your session expired, sign in again.
3.  Check for any error messages in the browser console (if you have developer tools access).

Try a different browser or clear cache:

1.  Clear your browser cache and cookies.
2.  Try using a different browser or incognito/private mode.
3.  Disable browser extensions that might interfere with the assistant.

If the problem persists, contact your administrator or support team.

## AI Assistant Gives Incorrect or Incomplete Answers

If the assistant provides answers that donât match your expectations:

Provide more context:

1.  Include specific names, IDs, or identifiers in your questions.
2.  Mention the environment, business group, or scope if relevant.
3.  Add details about what youâre trying to accomplish.

Example:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Instead of</th><th>Try</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>"Show me APIs"</p></td><td><p>"Show me production APIs in the Customer Services business group"</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>"Why isnât this working?"</p></td><td><p>"Why is the rate limit policy on my Payment API instance not being enforced?"</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>"Check errors"</p></td><td><p>"Show me error details for the User Authentication Agent over the last 24 hours"</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

Rephrase your question:

1.  Try asking the same question using different words.
2.  Break complex questions into smaller, focused questions.
3.  Use follow-up questions to narrow down the information you need.

Verify the assistant has access to the data:

1.  Check that the service is registered in your portfolio.
2.  Verify that monitoring or governance data has been collected for the service.
3.  Confirm that you have permissions to view the data youâre asking about.

## AI Assistant Suggests Actions I Canât Perform

If the assistant suggests actions but you canât perform them:

Check your permissions:

1.  Verify your Access Management permissions in
    [Enhanced Experience Permissions](exp-home-start#permissions).
2.  Ask your administrator to review your role assignments.
3.  The assistant may suggest actions available in the product, but your specific permissions
    determine what you can do.

Check your subscription tier:

1.  Verify whether you have API Portfolio Access or Agent + API Portfolio Access.
2.  Some features (like agents, MCP servers, LLM proxies) require Agent + API Portfolio Access.
3.  Contact your administrator or account team about upgrading your subscription if needed.

Check service state:

1.  The service may need to be in a specific state (active, registered, etc.) before certain actions
    are available.
2.  Verify that required prerequisites are met (for example, a gateway must be configured before
    creating instances).

## Confirmation Prompts Donât Appear

If the assistant describes an action but doesnât show a confirmation prompt:

Check for permissions issues:

1.  The assistant may detect that you donât have permission and skip the confirmation.
2.  Review any messages from the assistant explaining why the action canât be performed.

Verify the action type:

1.  Some responses are informational only and donât require confirmation.
2.  Only actions that navigate, create, update, or delete data show confirmation prompts.

Refresh and retry:

1.  Refresh the page and try the request again.
2.  Rephrase your request more explicitly: "Apply a rate limit policy to my API instance."

## Form Assistance Doesnât Work

If the assistant canât help fill in forms:

Check that a form is open:

1.  Form assistance only works when you have an active form visible on the page.
2.  Navigate to the form (for example, "Add Instance" or "Create Governance Strategy") before asking
    for help.

Be specific about what you want:

1.  Tell the assistant exactly what values to use: "Set the instance URL to
    [https://api.example.com/v1."](https://api.example.com/v1.)
2.  Provide all required information for the form fields.

Confirm changes manually:

1.  Review the confirmation banner carefully before accepting form changes.
2.  Remember that form assistance only fills in fieldsâyou still need to submit the form.

## Search Results Arenât Relevant

If search results donât match what youâre looking for:

Use more specific terms:

1.  Include the service type: "Find agents that handle payments" instead of "Find payment services."
2.  Specify attributes: "Show me REST APIs with OAuth policies" instead of "Show me APIs."

Check the scope:

1.  Verify youâre searching in the right business group or environment.
2.  Ask the assistant to filter: "Show me only production services."

Verify service metadata:

1.  Search results depend on how services are described in their metadata.
2.  If a service doesnât appear, check whether its description includes the terms youâre searching
    for.
3.  Update service descriptions to improve future search results.

## Suggestions Arenât Showing

If suggestion chips donât appear after the assistant responds:

Check response completeness:

1.  Wait for the assistantâs full response to complete.
2.  Suggestions appear after the assistant finishes streaming the response.

Check context:

1.  Suggestions are context-aware. If no relevant suggestions exist for your current situation, the
    assistant may not show any.
2.  Try asking a more specific question that has clear follow-up actions.

Refresh the conversation:

1.  Scroll to the bottom of the chat panel to ensure suggestions are visible.
2.  If the chat panel is too small, expand it or use the full-screen AI assistant page.

## AI Assistant Is Slow to Respond

If the assistant takes a long time to respond:

Check current load:

1.  During peak usage times, responses may be slower.
2.  If using monitoring or cost queries, large datasets may take longer to process.

Simplify your question:

1.  Break complex questions into smaller parts.
2.  Ask for summaries first, then drill into details.

Check your connection:

1.  Slow network connections can delay streaming responses.
2.  Try refreshing the page or reconnecting to your network.

## AI Assistant Isnât Visible

If you canât find the AI assistant panel or icon:

Check the page layout:

1.  Look for an icon on the right side of the screen or in the main navigation.
2.  Try different screen sizesâsome layouts may move the assistant to different locations.

Check feature availability:

1.  Verify that the AI assistant is enabled for your organization.
2.  Ask your administrator if the feature is available in your business group.

Check browser compatibility:

1.  Ensure youâre using a supported browser version.
2.  Update your browser if necessary.
3.  Disable browser extensions that might hide UI elements.

## AI Assistant Conversation History Is Lost

If your conversation history disappears:

Session expiration:

1.  Conversation history is tied to your session. If your session expires, history may be cleared.
2.  Sign in again and start a new conversation.

Browser storage:

1.  Clearing browser cache or cookies may remove conversation history.
2.  Conversation history is stored locally in your browser for the current session.

Intentional behavior:

1.  For privacy and security, conversation history may not persist across sessions.
2.  Sensitive information isnât stored long-term.

## Actions Performed by AI Assistant Fail

If the assistant confirms an action but the action fails:

Review error messages:

1.  The assistant should display any error messages from the system.
2.  Look for specific error details (validation errors, permission issues, etc.).

Check prerequisites:

1.  Verify that all required fields and configurations are complete.
2.  For instance creation, ensure the target gateway is available.
3.  For policy application, ensure the service instance is active.

Verify data:

1.  Double-check that URLs, names, and identifiers are correct.
2.  Ensure referenced services, gateways, or environments exist.

Try manually:

1.  Perform the action manually through the UI to see if the same error occurs.
2.  This helps determine whether the issue is with the AI assistant or the underlying system.

## AI Assistant Canât Access Recent Changes

If the assistant doesnât recognize recently registered services or configuration changes:

Wait for indexing:

1.  Newly registered services may take a few minutes to appear in search results.
2.  Refresh the page and try again after a few minutes.

Verify registration completed:

1.  Check that the service registration or configuration change was saved successfully.
2.  Look for confirmation messages or check the relevant catalog.

Be specific:

1.  Use exact service names or IDs rather than relying on search.
2.  For example: "Show me details for service ID 12345."

## Getting Additional Help

If these troubleshooting steps donât resolve your issue:

1.  Check with your administrator.

    Your administrator can verify your permissions, subscription tier, and feature availability.

2.  Review documentation.

    See [Use the AI Assistant (Ask MuleSoft)](exp-ai-assistant-use) for complete AI assistant usage
    guidance.

3.  Review system status.

    Ask your administrator to check whether there are any known issues or maintenance windows.

4.  Collect diagnostic information.

    Before contacting support, collect: \* The exact question or request you sent to the
    assistant \* The assistantâs response \* Any error messages displayed \* The page you were on
    when the issue occurred \* Your browser type and version

5.  Contact support.

    Reach out to your internal IT support or MuleSoft support with the diagnostic information.

## See Also

- [Use the AI Assistant (Ask MuleSoft)](exp-ai-assistant-use)
- [Get Started with the Enhanced Experience](exp-home-start)
- [Enhanced MuleSoft Experience Overview](exp-overview)
---

# Use the AI Assistant (Ask MuleSoft)

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

The enhanced experience includes an AI assistant called Ask MuleSoft that helps you navigate,
search, and manage your portfolio through natural language conversations. Access the assistant from
any page to get guidance, perform tasks, and discover features without navigating through menus.

## What Ask MuleSoft Can Do

The AI assistant provides context-aware help based on where you are in the enhanced experience:

Portfolio Management

- Search for agents, APIs, MCP servers, LLM proxies, and gateways.
- Get details about specific services.
- Find services by capability or description.
- Register new services with guided workflows.
- Create and manage instances.

Governance

- Create and manage governance strategies.
- Review conformance reports.
- Apply policies to services and instances.
- Check compliance status.
- Configure automated policies.

Monitoring and Observability

- Review performance metrics for services.
- Analyze error rates and latency trends.
- Check service health and availability.
- Set up alerts and notifications.
- View monitoring dashboards.

Cost Management

- Review token usage and spending.
- Identify cost optimization opportunities.
- Analyze usage patterns.
- Get recommendations for reducing costs.
- Apply cost control policies.

Navigation and Discovery

- Navigate to specific pages and catalogs.
- Find features and capabilities.
- Get step-by-step guidance for tasks.
- Learn about enhanced experience features.

Platform Configuration

- Connect and manage providers.
- Configure scanners for service discovery.
- Set up integrations (Slack, Claude Desktop).
- Manage business group settings.

## Before You Begin

Before using the AI assistant, make sure you have:

- An Anypoint Platform account with access to the enhanced experience
- Mule Developer Generative AI User permission to send prompts to the agent

  The AI assistant respects your Access Management permissions and can only perform actions youâre
  authorized to do. If you want the assistant to help with specific tasks like creating governance
  strategies or managing services, you also need the appropriate permissions for those actions.

  For more information, see [Enhanced Experience Permissions](exp-home-start#permissions).

## Access the AI Assistant

The AI assistant is available from every page in the enhanced experience.

1.  Look for the AI assistant icon or panel.
2.  Select the icon to open the chat panel.
3.  Type your question or request in the message box.
4.  Press Enter or select the send button.

The assistant responds with relevant information, suggestions, or actions you can take.

> [!TIP] You can also use the AI assistant as a dedicated full-screen page by clicking **Open full
> view** in the Ask MuleSoft menu.

## Ask Questions

Ask the AI assistant questions in natural language. The assistant understands context from the page
youâre on and provides relevant answers.

### Example Portfolio Questions

```text
Show me all agents in production
```

```text
Which APIs have governance violations?
```

```text
Find MCP servers that expose payment tools
```

```text
What instances are deployed to the staging gateway?
```

### Example Governance Questions

```text
What governance strategies are active?
```

```text
How do I create a new governance strategy?
```

```text
Show me all services with security violations
```

```text
Which policies are applied to my API instance?
```

### Example Monitoring Questions

```text
What's the error rate for my agent today?
```

```text
Show me latency trends for the last week
```

```text
Which services have the highest token usage?
```

```text
Are there any service health alerts?
```

### Example Cost Questions

```text
What's my total token spend this month?
```

```text
Which agents are using the most tokens?
```

```text
How can I reduce costs for my LLM proxies?
```

```text
Show me cost trends over time
```

### Example Navigation Questions

```text
How do I register a new API?
```

```text
Show me the governance strategies page
```

```text
Take me to monitoring for my agent
```

```text
Where do I configure scanners?
```

## Use Suggestions

After the AI assistant responds, it might show suggestion chips with related follow-up actions you
can take. If you donât see any suggestions, ask the assistant to suggest actions.

1.  Review the suggestion chips below the assistantâs response.
2.  Select a chip to ask that follow-up question or perform that action.
3.  The assistant executes the suggestion and shows the results.

Suggestions are context-aware and change based on:

- The page youâre currently viewing
- The question you just asked
- The services and features available to you
- Common next steps for your current task

> [!TIP] Suggestions help you discover related capabilities and complete multistep workflows more
> efficiently.

## Perform Actions with Confirmation

The AI assistant can perform actions on your behalf with your confirmation. When the assistant
proposes an action that modifies data or navigates to a new page, it shows a confirmation prompt.

### Navigation Actions

When the assistant wants to navigate you to a different page:

1.  The assistant shows a confirmation banner with the navigation target.
2.  Review where the assistant wants to take you.
3.  Select **Confirm** to navigate, or **Cancel** to stay on the current page.

    If you confirm, the page navigates to the destination.

### Data Modification Actions

When the assistant wants to create, update, or delete services or configurations:

1.  The assistant shows a confirmation banner with details of the proposed action.
2.  Review what the assistant will do.
3.  Select **Confirm** to execute the action, or **Cancel** to reject it.

    If you confirm, the assistant performs the action and shows the results.

Examples of actions that require confirmation:

- Creating new services (agents, APIs, MCP servers)
- Applying or removing policies
- Creating or modifying governance strategies
- Running or deleting scanners
- Creating or deleting alerts
- Updating service configurations

> [!IMPORTANT] Always review confirmation prompts carefully before approving actions. The AI
> assistant performs the exact action you confirm.

### Form Assistance

When you have a form open (such as creating an instance or configuring a policy), the assistant can
help fill in fields:

1.  Ask the assistant for help with the form (for example, "Fill in the instance details for my
    production API").
2.  The assistant proposes changes to specific form fields.

    A confirmation banner shows which fields will be updated and with what values.

3.  Review the proposed changes.
4.  Select **Confirm** to apply the changes to the form, or **Cancel** to reject them.

    If you confirm, the form fields update, but the form itself isnât submittedâyou still need to
    review and submit the form manually.

## Context-Aware Responses

The AI assistant tailors its responses based on:

Current Page

When you ask questions from a specific page (such as an API detail page or the monitoring
dashboard), the assistant prioritizes information relevant to that context.

Available Features

The assistant only suggests actions and features available to your subscription tier. If you have
API Portfolio Access, it focuses on API-related capabilities. If you have Agent + API Portfolio
Access, it includes agent-specific features.

Your Permissions

The assistant respects your Access Management permissions. It wonât suggest actions you donât have
permission to perform.

Active Services

When viewing a service detail page, the assistant knows which service youâre looking at and can
answer questions about that specific service.

Open Forms

When you have a form open, the assistant can help fill in form fields based on your requirements.

### Example: Context-Aware Help

On the APIs catalog page:

- Prompt: `Show me APIs with errors`
- Assistant: `Here are the APIs with recent errorsâ¦â`

On a specific API detail page:

- You: `Show me errors`
- Assistant: `Here are the recent errors for Payment Processing APIâ¦â`

## Search with Natural Language

Use the AI assistant to search your portfolio using natural language descriptions:

Semantic Search

Describe what youâre looking for conceptually, and the assistant finds matching services based on
capabilities and descriptions, not just exact name matches.

```text
Find services that handle payments
```

```text
Show me agents that use GPT-4
```

```text
Which APIs expose customer data?
```

```text
Find MCP servers for database operations
```

Filtered Search

Ask for services with specific characteristics:

```text
Show me production APIs with rate limiting policies
```

```text
Find staging agents with high token usage
```

```text
Which services don't have governance strategies applied?
```

```text
Show me APIs registered in the last week
```

## Get Guided Workflows

Ask the AI assistant to guide you through complex tasks:

```text
How do I create a governance strategy for my APIs?
```

```text
Walk me through registering a new agent
```

```text
Help me set up monitoring for my MCP server
```

```text
Guide me through connecting a provider
```

The assistant provides step-by-step instructions and can perform actions at each step with your
confirmation.

## Troubleshooting with the AI Assistant

The AI assistant can help diagnose and resolve issues:

```text
Why isn't my policy being enforced?
```

```text
My instance shows a connection error, what should I check?
```

```text
Why can't I see monitoring data for my service?
```

```text
My scanner failed, how do I fix it?
```

The assistant reviews your configuration, identifies potential issues, and suggests fixes.

## Best Practices

Follow these practices for effective interactions with the AI assistant:

Be Specific

Instead of "Show me APIs," try "Show me production APIs with governance violations."

Provide Context

If asking about a specific service, mention its name: "Whatâs the error rate for Payment Processing
API?"

Use Follow-Up Questions

Build on previous responses with follow-up questions like "Show me more details" or "Apply a rate
limit policy to that instance."

Review Confirmations

Always review confirmation prompts carefully before approving actions.

Use Suggestions

Explore the suggestion chips to discover related capabilities and next steps.

Ask for Help

If youâre unsure how to do something, ask: "How do Iâ¦â" or "Show me how toâ¦â"

Correct Mistakes

If the assistant misunderstands, clarify: "No, I meant the staging environment, not production."

## Limitations

Be aware of these limitations when using the AI assistant:

- The assistant canât perform actions outside the enhanced experience or access external systems
  directly.
- Some actions require specific permissions. If you donât have permission, the assistant informs you
  but canât grant permissions.
- The assistant works with data available in the enhanced experience. For services not yet
  registered in your portfolio, the assistant has limited information.
- Complex multistep workflows may require you to confirm each step individually.
- The assistant canât modify Access Management permissions or subscription tiers.

## Privacy and Security

The AI assistant operates within your organizationâs security and privacy controls:

- All interactions respect your Access Management permissions.
- The assistant only accesses data you have permission to view.
- Conversations are associated with your user session and arenât shared with other users.
- The assistant doesnât store sensitive data like API keys or credentials in conversation history.
- All actions performed by the assistant are logged and auditable.

## Tips for Advanced Use

Combine with Manual Actions

Use the assistant for discovery and research, then perform detailed configuration manually when you
need precise control.

Explore Capabilities

Ask "What can you help me with?" to learn about available features for your current context.

Check Status

Regularly ask for summaries: "Show me my portfolio health status" or "What governance issues need
attention?"

Automate Repetitive Tasks

Use the assistant for tasks you perform frequently, like checking monitoring data or reviewing
compliance reports.

Learn Features

Ask the assistant to explain features: "What is tool mapping?" or "How do governance strategies
work?"

## See Also

- [Troubleshoot the AI Assistant](exp-ai-assistant-troubleshoot)
- [Get Started with the Enhanced Experience](exp-home-start)
- [Enhanced MuleSoft Experience Overview](exp-overview)
- [View Your Portfolio Overview](exp-portfolio-overview)
- [Monitoring Your Services](exp-services-monitoring)
- [Working with Governance Strategies](exp-governance-work-with-strategies)
